perturbation.original_text: 2. Is made publicly available after its disclosure to me/us other than by any act or omission by us.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Is made publicly available after its disclosure to me/us by any act or omission by us.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction: previously, public information *not* disclosed by the recipient was exempt from confidentiality. Now, *any* publicly available information is exempt, regardless of who disclosed it. This weakens the confidentiality agreement but also introduces potential conflict with the primary confidentiality clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I/We (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) of (Address) hereby agree to keep confidential any information which has already or may be disclosed to us by: - (b) (Inventor’s name) of (c) (Address) Concerning the (d) (Insert brief description of invention) and we will not use it for our own benefit or disclose it to any other party without the written approval of: (b) (Inventor’s name) This obligation of confidentiality and non- use does not apply to information which: 1. Was in our possession before the Inventor disclosed it to me/us. 2. Is made publicly available after its disclosure to me/us by any act or omission by us. 3. Becomes known to us after its disclosure by (b) (Inventor’s name) from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality to (b) (Inventor’s name) Accepted for and on behalf of (a) (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) Signed Dated Signed Dated Signed Dated © E.V. Buchanan 2003 CLIENTS OF THE ABOVE PRACTICE MAY COPY THIS DOCUMENT FOR THEIR OWN USE P.L. Berry & Associates Ltd PATENT ATTORNEYS P O Box 1250, Christchurch 8140 Phone (03) 366-2761, Fax (03) 379-5744 Email: office@plberry.co.nz
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the condition that the public disclosure must *not* be caused by the recipient to be exempt from confidentiality. The original text specified that public availability caused by actions *other* than the recipient's own did not trigger a loss of confidentiality. The change, however, now states that *any* public availability, even if caused by the recipient's own actions, exempts the information from confidentiality. This directly contradicts the intent of the contradicted text, which implies confidentiality even if the recipient makes the information public. Therefore, the modified text creates a strong, direct contradiction that significantly weakens the confidentiality clause and creates uncertainty about the recipient's obligations, and adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Becomes known to us after its disclosure by (b) (Inventor’s name) from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality to (b) (Inventor’s name)
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Becomes known to us after its disclosure by (b) (Inventor’s name) from a third party who is under obligation of confidentiality to (b) (Inventor’s name)
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Originally, information received from a third party *not* bound by confidentiality was exempt. Now, the exemption applies *only* if the third party *is* bound by confidentiality, which seems counterintuitive and contradicts the goal of limiting confidential information. It now conflicts with the broad initial statement of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I/We (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) of (Address) hereby agree to keep confidential any information which has already or may be disclosed to us by: - (b) (Inventor’s name) of (c) (Address) Concerning the (d) (Insert brief description of invention) and we will not use it for our own benefit or disclose it to any other party without the written approval of: (b) (Inventor’s name) This obligation of confidentiality and non- use does not apply to information which: 1. Was in our possession before the Inventor disclosed it to me/us. 2. Is made publicly available after its disclosure to me/us other than by any act or omission by us. 3. Becomes known to us after its disclosure by (b) (Inventor’s name) from a third party who is under obligation of confidentiality to (b) (Inventor’s name) Accepted for and on behalf of (a) (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) Signed Dated Signed Dated Signed Dated © E.V. Buchanan 2003 CLIENTS OF THE ABOVE PRACTICE MAY COPY THIS DOCUMENT FOR THEIR OWN USE P.L. Berry & Associates Ltd PATENT ATTORNEYS P O Box 1250, Christchurch 8140 Phone (03) 366-2761, Fax (03) 379-5744 Email: office@plberry.co.nz
perturbation.justification: The original text states that information received from a third party NOT under obligation of confidentiality is exempt from the confidentiality agreement. The changed text now states that the exemption applies ONLY if the third party IS under obligation of confidentiality. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates an inconsistency within the document because it changes when the confidentiality obligation applies, contradicting the initial broad confidentiality statement and the previous exemption.

perturbation.original_text: Accepted for and on behalf of (a) (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) Signed Dated Signed Dated Signed Dated
perturbation.changed_text: Accepted for and on behalf of (a) (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) Signed Dated or Verbally Agreed
perturbation.explanation: The change adds 'or Verbally Agreed' creating a direct contradiction with the earlier clause stating that disclosure requires written approval, introducing uncertainty as to how acceptance can occur and making enforcement more complicated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I/We (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) of (Address) hereby agree to keep confidential any information which has already or may be disclosed to us by: - (b) (Inventor’s name) of (c) (Address) Concerning the (d) (Insert brief description of invention) and we will not use it for our own benefit or disclose it to any other party without the written approval of: (b) (Inventor’s name) This obligation of confidentiality and non- use does not apply to information which: 1. Was in our possession before the Inventor disclosed it to me/us. 2. Is made publicly available after its disclosure to me/us other than by any act or omission by us. 3. Becomes known to us after its disclosure by (b) (Inventor’s name) from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality to (b) (Inventor’s name) Accepted for and on behalf of (a) (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) Signed Dated or Verbally Agreed
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'or Verbally Agreed' as an alternative to 'Signed Dated' in the acceptance clause. The contradicted text explicitly states that any disclosure requires 'written approval'. The addition of 'or Verbally Agreed' directly contradicts this requirement of written approval, creating an in-text contradiction as it now allows for a potentially non-written form of acceptance, which is at odds with the requirement of written approval for disclosure.

perturbation.changed_text: Email: office@plberry.co.nz. This agreement is not legally binding.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the sentence 'This agreement is not legally binding' creates a direct and fundamental contradiction, invalidating the entire purpose of the agreement and conflicting with all preceding clauses regarding confidentiality and non-disclosure. The previous sentences and all actions are based on the agreement being binding; this change negates that basis.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I/We (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) of (Address) hereby agree to keep confidential any information which has already or may be disclosed to us by: - (b) (Inventor’s name) of (c) (Address) Concerning the (d) (Insert brief description of invention) and we will not use it for our own benefit or disclose it to any other party without the written approval of: (b) (Inventor’s name) This obligation of confidentiality and non- use does not apply to information which: 1. Was in our possession before the Inventor disclosed it to me/us. 2. Is made publicly available after its disclosure to me/us other than by any act or omission by us. 3. Becomes known to us after its disclosure by (b) (Inventor’s name) from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality to (b) (Inventor’s name) Accepted for and on behalf of (a) (Insert name of person or company to whom information is being disclosed) Signed Dated Signed Dated Signed Dated © E.V. Buchanan 2003 CLIENTS OF THE ABOVE PRACTICE MAY COPY THIS DOCUMENT FOR THEIR OWN USE P.L. Berry & Associates Ltd PATENT ATTORNEYS P O Box 1250, Christchurch 8140 Phone (03) 366-2761, Fax (03) 379-5744 Email: office@plberry.co.nz. This agreement is not legally binding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'This agreement is not legally binding' to the email address. This directly contradicts the implied and intended purpose of the entire document, including the 'Contradicted Text' section which outlines confidentiality obligations. By stating the agreement isn't legally binding, it invalidates the confidentiality agreement, causing a clear and direct conflict and making it unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction as different sections (the changed text and the contradicted text) provide conflicting obligations (being bound by confidentiality vs. the agreement being not legally binding).
